petitioner_background: The petitioner sought classification as an employment-based immigrant under section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act as an alien of extraordinary ability in the sciences. No specific details about the petitioner's field, nationality, or career qualifications are provided in the decision text. The petition was filed at the Vermont Service Center (EAC 03 139 52257).
outcome_reasoning: The appeal was summarily dismissed because counsel failed to identify specifically any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact for the appeal, as required by 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v). On Form I-290B, counsel stated only that 'The Government erred as a matter of fact and law' and indicated that a brief and/or additional evidence would be submitted within 30 days. The appeal was dated December 17, 2004, but over eight months later the AAO had received nothing further from counsel. Because counsel did not specifically identify any reason for the appeal and did not provide any additional evidence, summary dismissal was required.
legal_citations: Section 203(b)(1)(A) of the Immigration and Nationality Act
fraud_or_procedural_issues: Procedural dismissal: Counsel filed Form I-290B stating only that 'The Government erred as a matter of fact and law' and promised to submit a brief and/or additional evidence within 30 days, but failed to do so for over eight months. The appeal was summarily dismissed under 8 C.F.R. § 103.3(a)(1)(v) for failure to specifically identify any erroneous conclusion of law or statement of fact.
